payload.thought: I now have comprehensive coverage. Let me verify the key numbers with a quick calculation on the price move and the ex-H20 margin picture.

I now have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the key findings:

**FY26Q1 Results (reported May 28, 2025):**
- Revenue: $44.1B, up 12% Q/Q, up 69% Y/Y (beat consensus ~$43.3B)
- Data Center: $39.1B, up 10% Q/Q, up 73% Y/Y
- H20 charge: $4.5B (within the $5.5B flagged range)
- GAAP gross margin: 60.5%; Non-GAAP: 61.0%; **Ex-H20 charge: 71.3%**
- Non-GAAP EPS ex-H20: $0.96

**Q2 FY26 Guidance:**
- Revenue: $45.0B ±2% — explicitly reflects ~$8B lost H20 revenue
- Non-GAAP gross margin: 72.0% ±50bps; working toward mid-70% range late FY26
- Blackwell Ultra (GB300) introduced; ramping in H2 2025

**Demand/Supply:**
- Hyperscaler 2025 capex intact: $300B+ combined (Meta raised to $64-72B)
- Top 4 cloud providers ordered 3.6M Blackwell GPUs in 2025
- TSMC expanding CoWoS capacity; supply-chain bottlenecks easing
- Blackwell was fastest ramp ever ($11B in Q4 FY25)

**Bear Case:**
- H20 ban removes most China data-center business (~$8B/quarter revenue loss)
- Gross margin compression: 78.4% → 60.5% GAAP (71.3% ex-H20)
- Capex digestion risk in 2026: GPU fleets depreciating on 5-6yr schedules vs thin AI app revenue
- Customer concentration: individual customers repeatedly crossing 10% of revenue
- TD Cowen found MSFT walked away from ~2GW of data-center projects

**Price Action:**
- Stock closed at $139.00 on May 29 (day after earnings), up ~3.2% from $134.62 pre-earnings
- 60-day return: +28.4% (from $108.23)
- Strong rally into earnings (mid-May run from ~$113 to ~$135)

args.reason: Comprehensive evidence gathered: FY26Q1 results ($44.1B beat, $4.5B H20 charge, 71.3% ex-H20 margin), Q2 guidance ($45B with ~$8B H20 hit baked in), hyperscaler capex intact ($300B+), Blackwell ramp/GB300 roadmap, bear case (capex digestion, margin compression, China loss, customer concentration), and price action (+3.2% post-earnings, +28.4% over 60 days). Ready to write memo.
